identity.name: Assignment
identity.definition: An Assignment is a unit of instructional work — such as a practice set, reading task, activity, or project — created by a teacher and given to students within a class to practice, reinforce, or extend learning on specific skills, typically leveraging resources from the Renaissance content library.

representations.heading: Card layout
representations.description: How an Assignment appears in teacher dashboards and class activity feeds

attributes.name: Assignment Name/Title
attributes.description: Primary identifier. Appears in dashboards, class feeds, and student task lists.

attributes.name: Subject
attributes.description: Academic subject area: ELA, Math, Science, Social Studies, SEL.

attributes.name: Assignment Type
attributes.description: Practice, Reading, Activity, or Project. Determines UI treatment and completion flow.

attributes.name: Due Date
attributes.description: Optional deadline. Drives overdue status transitions and calendar views.

attributes.name: Status
attributes.description: Assigned, In Progress, Completed, Overdue, or Deleted. System-managed lifecycle state.

attributes.name: Product
attributes.description: Freckle, myON, Lalilo, or Nearpod. Determines content source and completion criteria.

attributes.name: Assignment Name/Title
attributes.description: Primary identifier. Appears in dashboards, class feeds, and student task lists.

attributes.name: Subject
attributes.description: Academic subject area: ELA, Math, Science, Social Studies, SEL.

attributes.name: Assignment Type
attributes.description: Practice, Reading, Activity, or Project. Determines UI treatment and completion flow.

attributes.name: Due Date
attributes.description: Optional deadline. Drives overdue status transitions and calendar views.

attributes.name: Status
attributes.description: Assigned, In Progress, Completed, Overdue, or Deleted. System-managed lifecycle state.

attributes.name: Product
attributes.description: Freckle, myON, Lalilo, or Nearpod. Determines content source and completion criteria.

attributes.name: Skill Targets
attributes.description: Skills this assignment is designed to practice or reinforce.

attributes.name: Class
attributes.description: The class this assignment is given to.

attributes.name: Resource(s)
attributes.description: Content items from the Renaissance library attached to this assignment.

attributes.name: Completion Criteria
attributes.description: Defines what constitutes completion: score threshold, time-on-task, items answered, pages read.

attributes.name: Created By
attributes.description: The teacher who created this assignment.

attributes.name: Created Date
attributes.description: Timestamp of assignment creation. System-generated, immutable.

attributes.name: Differentiation
attributes.description: Whether content is differentiated per student based on proficiency levels.

stories.title: Create and Assign Practice
stories.benefit: my students receive targeted practice on skills they need to develop, using content matched to their proficiency levels.
stories.whenClause: I create a new practice assignment and assign it to my class,
stories.thenClause: each student receives the assignment in their task list, optionally differentiated by proficiency level, with a clear due date and completion criteria.

stories.title: Complete Assignment
stories.benefit: my progress is recorded and my teacher can see how I performed on the targeted skills.
stories.whenClause: I finish all required items in an assignment and submit it,
stories.thenClause: a Score is generated, my skill proficiency is updated, and the assignment status changes to Completed.

stories.title: Monitor Assignment Progress
stories.benefit: I can identify students who are struggling and intervene before the due date.
stories.whenClause: I open an assignment's progress view,
stories.thenClause: I see each student's completion percentage, time spent, score (if completed), and can identify who hasn't started, who is in progress, and who is done.

businessRules.title: Ownership
businessRules.description: An Assignment is owned by the Teacher who created it. Only the creating teacher (or an Admin) can edit or delete it.

businessRules.title: Class Binding
businessRules.description: An Assignment must be associated with exactly one Class. All students enrolled in that class at the time of assignment receive it.

businessRules.title: Status Transitions
businessRules.description: Status follows the lifecycle: Assigned → In Progress (on first student action) → Completed (when completion criteria met) or → Overdue (when due date passes without completion). Any state can transition to Deleted by the teacher.

businessRules.title: Differentiation
businessRules.description: When differentiation is enabled, the system adjusts content difficulty per student based on their most recent proficiency data for the targeted skills.

businessRules.title: Score Generation
businessRules.description: A Score is generated for each student upon assignment completion. The score reflects performance on the targeted skills and feeds into the student's proficiency profile.

businessRules.title: Late Submission
businessRules.description: Students can submit overdue assignments. The assignment moves to Completed but retains the Overdue timestamp for teacher reporting.

businessRules.title: Duplication
businessRules.description: Duplicating an assignment creates a new instance with the same skill targets, resources, and completion criteria but a fresh status (Assigned), no scores, and a new created date.

states.name: Assigned
states.description: Created by teacher and distributed to class
states.triggers: Teacher creates and assigns to class

states.name: In Progress
states.description: At least one student has started working

states.name: Completed
states.description: All students have met completion criteria or teacher marks complete
states.triggers: All students submit, or teacher force-completes

states.name: Overdue
states.description: Due date has passed with incomplete submissions
states.triggers: System clock passes due date while status is Assigned or In Progress

states.name: Deleted
states.description: Removed by teacher; hidden from student views

relationships.mechanics: A teacher creates an Assignment and binds it to a Class. All students enrolled in the class receive the assignment.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-one. An Assignment belongs to exactly one Class; a Class can have many Assignments. Typical: 5-20 active assignments per class.
relationships.sorts: Assignments within a Class: by due date (default), by status, by subject, by creation date.
relationships.filters: By status (Assigned, In Progress, Completed, Overdue), by subject, by assignment type, by product.
relationships.dependencies: If a Class is archived, its Assignments are also archived. Students enrolled after assignment creation receive the assignment if still active.

relationships.mechanics: Students receive Assignments through their Class enrollment. Each student has an independent status and score per assignment.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-many (via Class). An Assignment reaches many Students; a Student has many Assignments. Typical: 15-35 students per assignment, 3-15 active assignments per student.
relationships.sorts: Students within an Assignment: by completion status (default), alphabetical, by score.
relationships.filters: By completion status (Not Started, In Progress, Completed), by score range, by time spent.
relationships.dependencies: If a Student is removed from a Class, their in-progress work on that class's Assignments is preserved but marked inactive.

relationships.mechanics: A Score is system-generated when a Student completes an Assignment. The score captures performance on the assignment's targeted skills.
relationships.cardinality: One-to-many. An Assignment generates one Score per Student upon completion. A Score belongs to exactly one Assignment and one Student.
relationships.sorts: Scores within an Assignment: by score value (default), by completion date, alphabetical by student.
relationships.filters: By score range, by proficiency level achieved, by completion date.
relationships.dependencies: If an Assignment is deleted, associated Scores persist for historical reporting but are no longer linked to an active assignment.

nestedObjects.name: Student

nestedObjects.name: Skill
nestedObjects.description: Skills this assignment targets for practice or reinforcement

nestedObjects.name: Resource

nestedObjects.name: Score
nestedObjects.description: System-generated when each student completes the assignment

allAttributes.name: Assignment Name/Title
allAttributes.description: Primary identifier. Appears in dashboards, class feeds, and student task lists.

allAttributes.description: Skills this assignment is designed to practice or reinforce. Drives differentiation and score attribution.

allAttributes.name: Class
allAttributes.description: The class this assignment is given to. Determines which students receive the assignment.

allAttributes.name: Due Date
allAttributes.description: Optional deadline. Drives overdue status transitions, calendar views, and student prioritization.

allAttributes.name: Status
allAttributes.description: Assigned, In Progress, Completed, Overdue, or Deleted. System-managed lifecycle state.

allAttributes.name: Assignment Type
allAttributes.description: Practice, Reading, Activity, or Project. Determines UI treatment, completion flow, and scoring model.

allAttributes.name: Subject
allAttributes.description: Academic subject area: ELA, Math, Science, Social Studies, SEL.

allAttributes.name: Product
allAttributes.description: Freckle, myON, Lalilo, or Nearpod. Determines content source, completion criteria, and scoring engine.

allAttributes.name: Resource(s)
allAttributes.description: Content items from the Renaissance library attached to this assignment.

allAttributes.name: Completion Criteria
allAttributes.description: Defines what constitutes completion: score threshold, time-on-task, items answered, or pages read.

allAttributes.name: Created By
allAttributes.description: The teacher who created this assignment. System-captured at creation time.

allAttributes.name: Created Date
allAttributes.description: Timestamp of assignment creation. System-generated, immutable.

allAttributes.name: Differentiation
allAttributes.description: Whether content is differentiated per student based on their most recent proficiency data.

structure.evidence: An Assignment has a rich attribute set: name, type, subject, due date, status, skill targets, completion criteria, differentiation flag, product, and more. You can design a detail page for an Assignment.

instances.evidence: Fraction Fundamentals Practice (Math, Freckle, due March 21), Charlotte's Web Reading Log (ELA, myON, due March 28), Phonics Blending Activity (ELA, Lalilo, no due date). Every class generates dozens of Assignment instances per grading period.

purpose.evidence: Assignments are the primary mechanism through which teachers deliver instructional practice to students. Teachers create them, students complete them, and the resulting Scores drive skill proficiency tracking and progress reporting.

sipValidation.verdict: Core activity object. The bridge between teacher instruction and student practice across all Renaissance content products.

synonyms.notes: Used in student task lists and completion screens
Specifically refers to the Practice assignment type in Freckle
Used when the assignment involves interactive or collaborative work
Overlaps with Assignment when teacher-directed content is delivered via the product
In AR, reading goals function as assignments with book-count or point targets
Generic term used in parent reports and notifications

objectViews.description: Active assignments for a class shown as cards with due dates, status, and progress. Teachers manage their class workload and check completion from here.
A student's assignments listed with due dates and completion status. Students see what's due and start or continue their work.
The complete assignment record — resources, skill targets, class, differentiation settings, and all management controls. Teachers create, edit, and track assignments from this page.
Assignments displayed as compact bars on a calendar view, showing due dates and status. Teachers scan their schedule to spot conflicts and plan ahead.
Assignments listed with status and due dates in a progress report. Teachers review completion patterns across students and identify overdue work.
The assignment shown as a section header within a class activity feed, grouping related student activity below it.
Assignments within a student's profile as a sortable table with status and progress. Teachers review what a student has been assigned and take action on individual items.
objectViews.userIntent: What assignments has this student been given?
